channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Elon Musk tells Republican lawmakers he's not to blame for federal firings
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s not his fault. Billionaire Elon Musk is telling Republican lawmakers that he is not to blame for the firings of thousands of federal workers, including veterans, as pushes to downsize the government.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans press Trump to resume military, intelligence aid to Ukraine
item.description: Republican lawmakers are starting to urge President Trump to reverse his decisions to pause U.S. military and intelligence assistance to Ukraine, warning that a prolonged stoppage of U.S. help for the war effort would have a seriously detrimental effect.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: War heroes and military firsts are among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — References to a World War II Medal of Honor recipient, the Enola Gay aircraft that dropped an atomic bomb on Japan and the first women to pass Marine infantry training are among the tens of thousands of photos and online posts marked for deletion as the Defense Department works to
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, LOLITA C. BALDOR and KEVIN VINEYS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Linda McMahon: Federal student aid could shift to another department
item.description: Newly confirmed Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon told NewsNation that student loans, Pell Grants and other federal aid "might best be served in another department.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Students rally at Florida capitol opposing amendment petition reform
item.description: From the Panhandle to the Keys, hundreds of students from across Florida protested Thursday at the state capitol.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social Security offices in NY set to close amid DOGE budget cuts
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Several Social Security offices in New York will be closing as the Department of Government Efficiency continues to cut federal spending. The closures come after the Social Security Administration said it found over $800 million in savings for the 2025 fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Transgender staffer navigates Texas Capitol with guarded care amid growing hostilities
item.description: Despite attempts to deny her existence, Mo Jenkins is steadfast in her public service mission to make life better for others.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: Censure resolutions: When to double down, and when to turn the page
item.description: The House voted Thursday to censure Rep. Al Green, D-Tex., for interrupting President Trump's Joint Address to Congress – but some of his colleagues think they should go further.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tensions flare in House over Al Green censure
item.description: Tensions flared in the House on Thursday as lawmakers voted to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his disruptive protest during President Trump’s address to Congress, prompting a dramatic scene on the House floor — and demands from Republicans for further retribution.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump changes course and delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday postponed 25 percent tariffs on many imports from Mexico and some imports from Canada for a month amid widespread fears of the economic fallout from a broader trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Protesters denounce ICE arrests at St. Louis Park manufacturing facility
item.description: Dozens of people gathered outside a St. Louis Park aluminum finishing facility Wednesday night to protest recent federal immigration arrests at the company.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox and Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs tariff exemptions for certain imports from Canada, Mexico
item.description: President Trump on Thursday signed off on tariff exemptions for imports from Canada and Mexico that are covered under a 2020 North American trade agreement, marking the latest reversal of duties imposed earlier this week. Under the orders, goods compliant with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP lawmakers balk at Trump’s call to repeal CHIPS Act
item.description: Republican lawmakers on Wednesday said President Trump’s call for Congress to “get rid of” the 2022 CHIPS and Science Act, which provided $52 billion for the domestic semiconductor manufacturing industry, is dead on arrival on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson chief of staff arrested on DUI charge after Trump address
item.description: Johnson’s office said they are aware of the incident and still confident in Haynes’ ability to lead the office team.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Veterans are speaking out on the Trump administration’s plans to cut the VA’s budget
item.description: A handful of veterans speaking to The Associated Press are divided over the Trump administration’s proposed cuts to the Department of Veterans Affairs. Stephen Watson is a former Marine who lives in Jesup, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: U.S. Sen. Tina Smith responds to Trump address, looming government shutdown
item.description: The Minnesota Democrat dissented along with her caucus. She joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer on Wednesday to talk about the president’s speech promoting his administration’s accomplishments so far.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LA Times to give op-eds AI-generated political rating, list of alternate views
item.description: The Los Angeles Times has launched an AI-driven "bias meter" to provide readers with alternative perspectives on opinion content.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, Fox 5

item.title: Inside Trump’s federal worker firings
item.description: Some of the removals have targeted a series of officials sure to conduct oversight of Trump’s actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: California lawmaker revives AI whistleblower protections concerns in new bill
item.description: The California lawmaker who introduced an AI safety protection bill later vetoed by Gov. Gavin Newsom has again introduced legislation to regulate the powerful technology. Sen.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Latex glove ban proposed in Nevada to help allergy sufferers
item.description: An allergy to latex can come with a lot of limits on where you can go and what you can do. Some clothing, medical procedures, and even visits to restaurants become potential problems.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
